decision_policy.adoption_style: adopts when the payoff is concrete and the risk feels bounded; prefers gradual integration over big-bang changes
trust_requirements: clear before-and-after value demonstrated in first 7 days
low-friction onboarding that doesn't require IT involvement
explicit data retention and deletion policies
rejection_triggers: extra admin burden during setup or migration
vague claims without proof or peer validation
subscription cost that grows before value is obvious
forced account linking to unverified third parties
proof_requirements: specific examples from similar field coordination roles
a short trial workflow with real but anonymised data
simple ROI signal within the first week (time saved or errors reduced)
evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and manager approval
purchasing_constraints: prefers flat-rate or usage-based pricing over tiered upsells
expects immediate export/delete functionality upon cancellation

communication_patterns: uses concise bullet points when outlining workflows or requirements
switches to Mandarin when discussing family logistics or personal admin matters
avoids marketing language; asks clarifying questions about data handling, onboarding steps, and off-ramps
prefers structured comparisons with clear pros/cons and real-world constraints noted

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: Enrichment preserves all identity anchors, locale, household structure, occupation, and fixed signals while layering in realistic trade-offs, constraints, and daily pressures. Added specifics around purchase approval mechanics, data privacy norms aligned with Singaporeâs operational context, bilingual communication patterns, and bounded trial expectations. Expanded behavioral cues to reflect how a mid-career operator with high schedule/caregiving load actually evaluates, tests, and rejects tools. Kept the persona actionable for product validation by focusing on JTBD, proof thresholds, decision blockers, and measurable success metrics without drifting into stereotyping or diagnostic framing.
